verses.text: Four kings of the east — Chedorlaomer of Elam and his allies — had ruled five kings of the Jordan plain for twelve years. In the thirteenth year, the local kings rebel. The chapter opens reading like ancient news.

Four kings of the east — Chedorlaomer of Elam and his allies — had ruled five kings of the Jordan plain for twelve years. In the thirteenth year, the local kings rebel. The chapter opens reading like ancient news.

Four kings of the east — Chedorlaomer of Elam and his allies — had ruled five kings of the Jordan plain for twelve years. In the thirteenth year, the local kings rebel. The chapter opens reading like ancient news.

Chedorlaomer's coalition sweeps southward, defeating ancient peoples whose names would mostly be forgotten — Rephaim, Zuzim, Emim, Horim, Amalekites, Amorites. Empire moves through the region with brutal momentum.

Chedorlaomer's coalition sweeps southward, defeating ancient peoples whose names would mostly be forgotten — Rephaim, Zuzim, Emim, Horim, Amalekites, Amorites. Empire moves through the region with brutal momentum.

Chedorlaomer's coalition sweeps southward, defeating ancient peoples whose names would mostly be forgotten — Rephaim, Zuzim, Emim, Horim, Amalekites, Amorites. Empire moves through the region with brutal momentum.
The five Jordan-plain kings meet them in the Vale of Siddim — a valley full of bitumen pits. Sodom and Gomorrah are defeated; their kings fall into the pits. The victors take all the food and goods.

The five Jordan-plain kings meet them in the Vale of Siddim — a valley full of bitumen pits. Sodom and Gomorrah are defeated; their kings fall into the pits. The victors take all the food and goods.
And they took Lot, Abram's brother's son, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed. Lot's choice of the well-watered plain has cost him everything.
News reaches Abram the Hebrew — the first time this word is used in Scripture. He arms his 318 trained servants, born in his own house. The man of altars is also a man of action.
News reaches Abram the Hebrew — the first time this word is used in Scripture. He arms his 318 trained servants, born in his own house. The man of altars is also a man of action.

By night Abram divides his force and strikes, pursuing the empire's army all the way to Hobah, north of Damascus. He recovers everything — Lot, the women, and the people. One man with a household army defeats four kings.

By night Abram divides his force and strikes, pursuing the empire's army all the way to Hobah, north of Damascus. He recovers everything — Lot, the women, and the people. One man with a household army defeats four kings.

The king of Sodom comes out to meet Abram. But before he can speak, Melchizedek — king of Salem and priest of the most high God — arrives with bread and wine. Hebrews and Psalms later point to him as a picture of Christ.

The king of Sodom comes out to meet Abram. But before he can speak, Melchizedek — king of Salem and priest of the most high God — arrives with bread and wine. Hebrews and Psalms later point to him as a picture of Christ.

Melchizedek blesses Abram in the name of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth. And Abram gives him a tenth of everything — the first tithe in Scripture, given by the patriarch before the law was ever written.

Melchizedek blesses Abram in the name of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth. And Abram gives him a tenth of everything — the first tithe in Scripture, given by the patriarch before the law was ever written.

The king of Sodom says: keep the goods, just give me the persons. Abram refuses every thread. I will not take from you so much as a shoelace, lest you say, I made Abram rich. Melchizedek gets the tithe; Sodom gets nothing.
